steps.title: Convert the percent
steps.content:
The maximum phosphorus mass is $0.002\%$ of the sample mass. Convert the percent to a decimal.
$$0.002\%=\frac{0.002}{100}=0.00002$$

steps.title: Multiply by the sample mass
steps.content:
Use the decimal form of the percent with the $160$-gram sample.
$$0.00002\cdot 160=0.0032$$
The maximum phosphorus mass is $0.0032$ grams. The answer is .0032.
